rules.rule: Always filter partition column to avoid full scan
Use order_status='delivered' for all customer/GMV metrics
Country filter differs per table — never mix
Live outlet ALWAYS needs all 3 filters: status='active' AND is_consumer_visible='1' AND outlet_deleted=0
rules.note: is_consumer_visible is STRING '1' not int. Inject even if user doesn't mention.

rules.rule: Default date = last 7 days if not specified
rules.sql: WHERE day BETWEEN DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(),INTERVAL 7 DAY) AND DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE(),INTERVAL 1 DAY)

rules.rule: is_bounce/is_moved live in session_segments_nat NOT nat_events_complete
For hub/city daily funnel use daily_hubz_rca_nat first (pre-agg, cheap); use nat_events_complete only for user/session-level detail
New + Repeat = Active Customers — validate if computing all three
Discount cols bank_funded/noon_funded/rest_funded/Channel_discount are NEGATIVE — use -1*SUM() or ABS(). Outlet_discount is positive.
Churn features always on delivered AE orders; country='ae' AND order_status='delivered' at base
outlet_status_complete and temp_outlet_status are snapshots — NO partition, always full scan. Never add day filter.
Inter-order gap: use order_placed_at (timestamp) not day (date)
food_order_item.price is STRING — CAST to NUMERIC before arithmetic
food_order_item is item-grain — use COUNT(DISTINCT order_nr) for orders
mpnoon_order_discount & mpnoon_food_order_discount are multi-row per order — COUNT(DISTINCT order_nr)
Always SAFE_DIVIDE for rates/ratios — never plain division
Pre-agg tables (daily_hubz_rca_nat, daily_rca_nat_reattributed, outlet_dod_funnel): use SUM() on metric cols, never COUNT()
Always SUM metrics then divide — never compute rates row-by-row
mp_order_base_mat requires ops_order_date filter — BigQuery enforces, query fails without it
mp_order_base_mat time cols (O2D,O2A,A2RFP,P2D,est_O2D) are in SECONDS — divide by 60 for minutes
daily_rca_nat_reattributed always needs country filter (grain=day+country); missing = doubled/mixed data
task_misc has no direct path to daily_orders — join via task
Cancel rate denominator = IN ('delivered','canceled') — never 'delivered' only (gives 0%)
Burn for RestoOwnDelivery=1 must include delivery_subsidy
'restaurants'→COUNT(DISTINCT restaurant_code); 'outlets/locations'→COUNT(DISTINCT Outlet_Code). Never use Outlet_Code to answer restaurant-count questions.
rules.bad: COUNT(DISTINCT Outlet_Code) for a restaurants question

rules.rule: When a brand name is mentioned (e.g. 'McDonald\'s', 'KFC', 'Starbucks'), default to restaurant-level aggregation (GROUP BY restaurant_code or restaurant_name) — NOT outlet-level. Only use outlet_code/outlet_name if the user explicitly asks for individual outlet breakdown.
rules.example: 'orders for McDonald\'s' → GROUP BY restaurant_code/restaurant_name; NOT GROUP BY outlet_code

rules.rule: Widget type lives in event_misc.$.wt as slash-delimited path: SPLIT($.wt,'/')[0]=page (hp/d1/d2/dp/so/sd), SPLIT($.wt,'/')[1]=widget (ol=outlet list, banner, carousel, etc). Always SPLIT before filtering on page or widget.

Food session filter in nat_events_complete (and raw_events_v2) requires OR condition — a session is food if $.szf is not null (food zone flag) OR $.mpe='food' (mini-page entry). Never use just one condition alone.
rules.good: AND (JSON_VALUE(event_misc,'$.szf') IS NOT NULL OR JSON_VALUE(event_misc,'$.mpe')='food')

rules.rule: Funnel metrics in nat_events_complete use COUNT(DISTINCT sid) per event_type via CASE WHEN for session-level counts. OC-level metrics use COUNT(DISTINCT JSON_EXTRACT_SCALAR(event_misc,'$.oc')). Always track BOTH — sid answers 'how many sessions', OC answers 'how many outlets touched'. Never conflate the two.
rules.good: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN event_type='outlet_impression_v2' THEN JSON_EXTRACT_SCALAR(event_misc,'$.oc') END) AS OC_Impression, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN event_type='outlet_impression_v2' THEN sid END) AS Session_Impression
rules.bad: COUNT(DISTINCT sid) for all metrics including outlet-level counts

rules.rule: Active restaurant filter on outlet_status_complete must always use all three conditions: bq_activation_status, outlet_deleted, and country. Never substitute with status='active' or is_consumer_visible alone.
rules.good: AND osc.bq_activation_status = 'Activated by Ops' AND osc.outlet_deleted = 0 AND LOWER(osc.country) = 'ae'

rules.rule: daily_orders_items has NO country column and NO order_status column. NEVER filter by country or order_status directly on this table. Always JOIN to daily_orders to apply those filters.
rules.good: FROM noonbifood.reporting.daily_orders_items doi JOIN noonbifood.reporting.daily_orders do ON doi.order_nr=do.order_id WHERE doi.order_date=... AND do.country='ae' AND do.order_status='delivered'
rules.bad: FROM noonbifood.reporting.daily_orders_items WHERE country='ae' OR order_status='delivered'

rules.rule: 'PID' always means id_partner_owner — NEVER restaurant_code. When a user says 'PID 491699', generate: WHERE id_partner_owner = 491699. One PID can span multiple restaurant_codes/brands.

'RID' always means id_restaurant (numeric) — NEVER restaurant_code. daily_orders has no id_restaurant column; filtering by RID requires JOIN to outlet_status_complete on restaurant_code. One RID = one restaurant brand.
rules.good: JOIN noonbifood.reporting.outlet_status_complete osc ON do.restaurant_code = osc.restaurant_code WHERE osc.id_restaurant = <rid>

rules.rule: Widget CASE must follow strict top-down precedence. (1) OL context check first — page+widget combined. (2) multiple_outlet_carousels_new sub-typed by full wt string for reorder/recommended. (3) Named exact carousels: homepage_carousel_featured, pre_search_ads_carousel, uae_hompage_new_cuisine_tiles, uae_homepage_newusptile_2. (4) Broad: %banner%. (5) Broad fallback: %carousel%. (6) 'others'. Never place %carousel% before named carousels — it silently absorbs Feature Bite, Pre Search, reorder, recommended into the wrong bucket with no error.
rules.good: CASE WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%ol%' AND page='hp' THEN 'HP - OL' WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%ol%' AND page='so' THEN 'SO - OL' WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%ol%' AND page='sd' THEN 'SD - OL' WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%ol%' THEN 'OL' WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%multiple_outlet_carousels_new%' AND lower(full_wt) LIKE '%reorder%' THEN 'Multiple_Carousel_Reorder' WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%multiple_outlet_carousels_new%' AND lower(full_wt) LIKE '%recommended%' THEN 'Multiple_Carousel_Recommended' WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%homepage_carousel_featured%' THEN 'Feature Bite' WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%pre_search_ads_carousel%' THEN 'Pre Search' WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%uae_hompage_new_cuisine_tiles%' THEN 'Cuisine Tile' WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%uae_homepage_newusptile_2%' THEN 'Usptile' WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%banner%' THEN 'Banner' WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%carousel%' THEN 'Carousel' ELSE 'others' END
rules.bad: CASE WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%carousel%' THEN 'Carousel' WHEN lower(widget) LIKE '%homepage_carousel_featured%' THEN 'Feature Bite' END -- broad carousel match fires first, swallowing Feature Bite and Pre Search

rules.rule: For multiple_outlet_carousels_new sub-typing, the reorder/recommended keyword must be checked against the FULL $.wt string (not just SAFE_OFFSET(1) or SAFE_OFFSET(2)), because the keyword appears in deeper path segments beyond slot 1. SAFE_OFFSET(2) may be NULL.
Always apply lower() to both the extracted $.wt value AND all LIKE pattern comparisons for page and widget slots. Raw $.wt values can be mixed case. Never compare without lowercasing first.

The Cuisine Tile source string contains a deliberate typo: 'uae_hompage_new_cuisine_tiles' (missing 'e' in homepage). Always use the typo in LIKE patterns. Correcting it to 'homepage' will return zero rows.

When a user asks for metrics from a named widget using natural language, always resolve to the canonical widget label before writing SQL. Never respond 'cannot identify widget' without first checking the alias map. Canonical mappings: 'reorder widget/carousel'→'Multiple_Carousel_Reorder'; 'recommended widget/carousel'→'Multiple_Carousel_Recommended'; 'feature bite/featured carousel'→'Feature Bite'; 'pre search/pre search ads'→'Pre Search'; 'cuisine tile'→'Cuisine Tile'; 'usp tile/usptile'→'Usptile'; 'ol widget/outlet list'→'OL/HP-OL/SO-OL/SD-OL'; 'banner'→'Banner'; 'carousel'→'Carousel (generic fallback only)'.

GMV and AOV cannot be computed from nat_events_complete alone — that table has NO GMV column. For any widget AOV/GMV metric: (1) use nat_events_complete to get outlet_codes from transaction_v2 events filtered by widget, (2) JOIN to daily_orders on outlet_code=restaurant_code AND day=day to get GMV. AOV = SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(do.GMV), COUNT(DISTINCT do.order_id)). The SELECT and SAFE_DIVIDE must reference daily_orders columns (do.GMV, do.order_id), not nat_events_complete columns.
rules.good: WITH widget_txns AS (SELECT JSON_EXTRACT_SCALAR(event_misc,'$.oc') AS outlet_code, day FROM noonbifood.growthv1.nat_events_complete WHERE event_type='transaction_v2' AND <widget_conditions>) SELECT t.day, SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(do.GMV), COUNT(DISTINCT do.order_id)) AS AOV FROM widget_txns t JOIN noonbifood.reporting.daily_orders do ON t.outlet_code=do.restaurant_code AND t.day=do.day WHERE do.order_status='delivered' AND do.country='ae' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
rules.bad_1: SELECT SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(GMV),...) FROM nat_events_complete -- GMV column does not exist in nat_events_complete
rules.bad_2: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT transactionId) FROM nat_events_complete -- this gives transaction count, not AOV

rules.description: When asked to benchmark/compare the restaurant-level or outlet-level metrics, always benchmark against the cuisine group derived from daily_orders. The cuisine benchmark serves as the reference peer group — never benchmark in isolation or against the overall platform average unless explicitly asked for

sql_guidance.step_1: Derive cuisine from daily_orders using main_cuisine for the same time window as the metric being computed.
sql_guidance.step_2: Compute the metric for all outlets/restaurants sharing the same main_cuisine value.
sql_guidance.step_3: Use that cuisine-level aggregate as the benchmark denominator or reference value.
sql_guidance.step_4: Present the entity metric alongside the cuisine benchmark — never present the metric alone.

rules.example_pattern: SELECT main_cuisine, COUNT(DISTINCT outlet_id) AS outlets, SUM(orders) AS orders, SAFE_DIVIDE(SUM(orders), SUM(sessions)) AS conversion_rate FROM noonbifood.reporting.daily_orders WHERE day BETWEEN @start AND @end GROUP BY main_cuisine
notes: main_cuisine must be sourced from daily_orders, not from outlet_status_complete, to reflect cuisine as seen in actual transactional data.
If a specific outlet has no orders in the window, it still belongs to the cuisine group of its most recent active orders.
Do not fall back to platform-wide averages when a cuisine group has fewer than 5 outlets — flag sparsity instead.

rules.rule: Restaurant names are NOT unique — the same name can map to multiple rcodes (e.g. 'KFC' has 20+ codes, 'Kfc' and 'KFC' are stored as separate entries). Never filter directly by restaurant_name alone. Always: (1) use LOWER() on both sides for case-insensitive matching, (2) first resolve the name to rcode(s) via a lookup on outlet_status_complete, (3) then filter downstream queries using rcode IN (...) — never by restaurant_name alone.
rules.good: SELECT DISTINCT restaurant_code FROM noonbifood.sales.outlet_status_complete WHERE LOWER(restaurant_name) = LOWER('KFC')

rules.title: Fuzzy matching for item/brand/product names
rules.description: When a user filters by item name, meal name, drink name, or brand name, always normalize both sides and use REGEXP_CONTAINS to catch all spacing/casing variants.

rules.rule: Always use LOWER() on both sides when comparing or filtering any string column. Never compare raw string columns directly — mixed case in data will cause missed rows.
rules.good: WHERE LOWER(city) = 'dxb' | WHERE LOWER(order_status) = 'delivered' | WHERE LOWER(restaurant_name) LIKE '%kfc%'
rules.bad: WHERE city = 'DXB' | WHERE order_status = 'Delivered' | WHERE restaurant_name LIKE '%KFC%'
rules.exceptions: Columns with documented fixed-case values where case is guaranteed: country='ae', is_consumer_visible='1', bq_activation_status='Activated by Ops'. Apply LOWER() for all other string comparisons.
